title: brassica oleracea var. rupestris
intro: Brassica oleracea var. rupestris, also known as Wild Cabbage or Sea Cabbage, is a type of wild mustard plant that grows in coastal areas and is a member of the Brassica family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and combat infections. The health benefits of Wild Cabbage include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and supporting digestive health, making it a valuable addition to a healthy diet. Wild Cabbage can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also edible and can be consumed raw or cooked, providing a rich source of vitamins and minerals.
